steps.title: Find archaeologists' purpose
steps.content: The text says scent is connected to routines, memories, and social events. Archaeologists hope to use recovered scents to understand ancient rituals, trade, social hierarchies, and medicine, so the interest is in what scents can reveal about past societies.

steps.title: Discard unsupported aims
steps.content: Choice A mentions a decline in smell, which the text does not discuss. Choice C focuses on present-day enjoyment, not archaeology. Choice D says archaeologists hope to develop new medicines, but medicine is listed as something to understand historically.

steps.title: State the ancient-life insight
steps.content: The remaining choice says recovered scents could illuminate important aspects of ancient life. The answer is B.
